title: fraxinus oregona var. latifolia
intro: Fraxinus oregona var. latifolia, commonly known as Oregon Ash or Green Ash, is a deciduous tree species native to the western United States and Canada. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Fraxinus oregona var. latifolia include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote digestion, as well as its use in treating colds, coughs, and other upper respiratory infections. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its bark and leaves can be used in traditional medicine, providing a natural remedy for various health concerns.
